# AgentCanvas

> A friendlier way to steer the app an AI is building for you. AgentCanvas keeps repo evidence and the display canvas separate, so an AI coding agent can turn code behavior into plain-English flows and only implement source changes when asked.

## What it is

AgentCanvas is a local, agent-agnostic tool. It indexes a workspace into `.agentcanvas/workflow.ir.json`, then the invoking agent turns that evidence into simple **journeys** in `.agentcanvas/canvas.ir.json`: **When / Do / If** steps with if/else branches. A person can edit a step ("add a step", "change what happens", "add a rule", "remove a step") in their own words. Canvas-only edits update the display canvas, not source code.

## Who it's for

Non-technical people (operators, founders, power users) who want to understand and steer an app an AI agent is building, without reading code.

## How an AI coding agent uses it

1. Start it: `agentcanvas start --workspace .` (install: `pip install use-agentcanvas`). It serves a local web canvas and prints a URL.
2. Read `.agentcanvas/workflow.ir.json` as raw evidence and write/update `.agentcanvas/canvas.ir.json` as the browser display canvas.
3. For canvas edits, update `.agentcanvas/canvas.ir.json` progressively. Do not re-index unless evidence needs to be refreshed.
4. For explicit source-code implementation requests, read `.agentcanvas/pending/`, implement the change in code, run the relevant tests, re-index with `agentcanvas index --workspace .` to refresh evidence, and report what changed.

Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Antigravity, or any agent that can read files and run commands. Copy-paste fallback is always available.

## Key facts

- Local-first, runs from the command line.
- Agent-agnostic (no vendor lock-in).
- `.agentcanvas/workflow.ir.json` is the raw index/evidence file.
- `.agentcanvas/canvas.ir.json` is the display canvas source of truth.
- Canvas edits are canvas changes, not direct code changes, unless the user explicitly asks for implementation.
